spec_match.rationale: Uses the lowercase tokens `"prefetchcache"` and `"prerendercache"` — the spec and Chrome ship the camelCase `"prefetchCache"` / `"prerenderCache"`. The header this page builds would be silently ignored by Chrome.

interactive.rationale: Populate / Clear / Navigate / Reset buttons drive cache lists, a header readout, and an event timeline — real interactivity past static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises three URLs and selective invalidation, which is delivered, but it also promises the user can `observe whether the next navigation results in a cache hit or miss` — which works, but the random URL picker undercuts the educational arc (you can't deliberately test the cleared entry).

edge_cases.rationale: Covers populate → clear → navigate-hit/miss, plus a `cache` directive note. Misses: the spec's auto-append of prefetchCache when `cache` or `*` is set, cross-origin scoping, active-prerender skip.

references.rationale: The page itself has no see-also section and the chromestatus link is absent. Only an info-box mentions Chrome 138. This violates invariant #3 (every page must include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link).

design_system.rationale: Variables used throughout, but a page-local `button` rule sets `background: var(--text-black); color: var(--bg-ivory)` and then a `.secondary` sets `background: var(--bg-ivory); color: var(--text-black)` — these duplicate global styles unnecessarily but don't break the contrast rule. The `dot-sm.hit` color uses color-mix with --accent-amber which may have contrast issues against the page background; needs a check.

openQuestions.title: Fix the lowercase token names
openQuestions.detail: The whole demo uses `"prefetchcache"` and `"prerendercache"` in the header builder, in the timeline messages, in the info-box copy, and inline in JS. The spec and Chrome implementation use camelCase: `"prefetchCache"` and `"prerenderCache"`. A response with the lowercase versions will be ignored. This is a major correctness bug — anyone copying the snippet will silently fail in production.

openQuestions.title: Add the missing chromestatus + spec references
openQuestions.detail: The page has no `see also` or `references` section. Per invariant #3 in CLAUDE.md, every concept page must include https://chromestatus.com/feature/4755745652080640. Add a see-also block with that link plus the WICG spec URL.

openQuestions.title: Let the user pick which URL to navigate to
openQuestions.detail: Random URL selection on Navigate makes it hard to deliberately test the cleared entry vs the alive entry. A dropdown or three buttons (one per URL) would make the demo deterministic and let visitors observe the rules instead of hoping the random pick lands.

openQuestions.title: Surface the auto-append for `cache` / `*`
openQuestions.detail: Per spec, encountering `cache` or `*` automatically also clears the prefetch cache. The page lists `cache` as a separate checkbox but neither triggers the prefetch clear nor explains the auto-append, leaving visitors with the wrong mental model.

openQuestions.title: Add a feature-detection probe
openQuestions.detail: Per the design-system rules in CLAUDE.md, OS- and engine-specific features benefit from a real-capability read. There is no `Response.headers.get('Clear-Site-Data')` direct check, but you can probe whether the user-agent string suggests Chrome >= 138 or use a known capability test (e.g. `'speculationrules' in HTMLScriptElement.supports`). Add a small live banner saying whether the host browser will honour the demo.

summary: The most broken of the four concepts on this feature: the lowercase token names are a correctness bug that would mislead anyone copying the snippet, and the page is missing the mandatory chromestatus reference link. Interactive shell is fine but the educational payload is currently wrong.
